title: rubus procerus subsp. lacertosus
intro: Rubus procerus subsp. lacertosus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or the Himalayan bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus genus, a type of thorny shrub native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus procerus subsp. lacertosus include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as its potential to help manage conditions such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made by drying the leaves and roots, then combining them with other herbs to create a medicinal concoction.
